data.title: Visa unveils agentic payment for businesses
data.description:
Visa (V) introduced Intelligent Commerce Connect, a technology that allows businesses to connect and participate in AI-powered commerce, the company said on Wednesday. Payments through the new offering aren't tied to Visa cards, and it allows payments over several major protocols.

Through a single integration via the Visa Acceptance Platform, Intelligent Commerce Connect enables secure payment initiation, tokenization, spend controls, and authentication, it added. The new feature is currently in pilot with select partners, including Aldar, AWS, Diddo, Highnote, Mesh, Payabli, and Sumvin. More partners will be added this year.

The solution integrates both Visa Intelligent Commerce APIs, which are used to process agent purchases using Visa cards, and other networks' APIs, allowing agents to pay with both Visa (V) and non-Visa cards. 

The system works with major token vault providers so that agent platforms can plug into existing credential infrastructure and avoid being locked into a single token vault/vendor. It also allows agent-initiated payments through several major protocols, including Trusted Agent Protocol, Machine Payments Protocol, Agentic Commerce Protocol, and Universal Commerce Protocol.

Visa (V) stock rose 2.1% in regular session trading on Wednesday.

data.title: AeroVironment receives $25M contract from U.S. Air Force for technology development
data.description:
The United States Air Force awarded a three-year, $25 million contract to UES, a division of AeroVironment (AVAV), to help transition health and performance technologies from research into operational use.

The work will support the Air Force Research Laboratory 711th Human Performance Wing, which focuses on improving the effectiveness and resilience of military personnel.

The contract is aimed at advancing technologies that have remained in mid-stage development, typically classified between Technology Readiness Levels 3 and 5, AeroVironment said Tuesday. These include sensors, diagnostic tools and advanced materials that have shown promise but have not yet been widely deployed in operational settings.

According to the company, the effort will concentrate on refining these technologies so they can be used in real-world military environments, where conditions are often harsh and access to infrastructure is limited.

A company executive said that devices designed for military use face additional hurdles compared with commercial health technologies, citing environmental constraints and strict operational requirements as barriers to adoption.

Sensors, wearables, data analytics

The scope of the contract covers four main areas of development. These include sensor systems designed to integrate with aircraft and monitor pilot conditions, as well as wearable diagnostic tools intended for use in remote or austere environments.

The program will also focus on developing data platforms that use artificial intelligence and machine learning to analyze biosensor data and generate actionable insights. In addition, researchers will explore emerging biotechnology approaches, such as engineered biological sensors and stress-related interventions.

Johnathan Jones, senior vice president of cyber and mission solutions at the company, said in a statement that the integration of biosensing technologies with advanced analytics is expected to play a role in shaping how military readiness is monitored and managed.

AeroVironment (AVAV) has previously worked with the 711th Human Performance Wing on projects including onboard oxygen monitoring systems designed to investigate unexplained physiological events affecting pilots.

The new contract is intended to expand on that work by improving the durability of hardware, accelerating data processing and refining user interfaces to meet operational needs across the Air and Space Force.

data.title: Marvel Biosciences plans convertible debenture private placement
data.description:
• Marvel Biosciences (MRVL:CA) intends to raise up to $500K via a non-brokered private placement of unsecured convertible debentures.
  • The debentures will carry a 12% annual interest rate and mature on December 31, 2027.
  • Interest may be paid in cash or shares.
  • Debentures are convertible into common shares at $0.12 per share at the holder’s option.
  • The company can force conversion if its share price reaches $0.60 for at least 10 consecutive trading days.

data.title: Belgium selects Cessna SkyCourier for special operations fleet
data.description:
Belgium chose the Cessna SkyCourier aircraft for its Special Operations Forces, placing an order for five aircraft in a move that introduces the platform into military service for the first time.

 

 

The aircraft will be supplied by Textron Aviation, a subsidiary of Textron (TXT), with deliveries scheduled to begin in 2027. Terms of the agreement weren't disclosed.

Aircraft to support transport and crisis missions

Belgium plans to use the SkyCourier fleet for a range of missions, including transporting personnel and equipment, as well as supporting logistics, medical evacuation and crisis response operations.

The aircraft will first be delivered to Sabena Engineering, which will carry out modifications and certification work in Belgium before transferring the planes to the country’s Special Operations Forces.

First military adoption for the platform

The selection represents the first confirmed military use of the SkyCourier, a twin-engine turboprop aircraft designed for cargo and utility roles. Travis Tyler, president and chief executive, Textron Aviation Defense, said in a statement that the decision indicates growing interest from armed forces in using the aircraft for demanding operational environments.

The executive pointed to the aircraft’s ability to operate from short or less-developed runways, along with its cost profile, as factors contributing to its appeal for military customers.

Local industry role in modifications

Sabena Engineering will be responsible for integrating mission-specific systems and completing certification work within Belgium. The company’s chief executive said the arrangement allows the aircraft to be tailored to operational needs while supporting domestic aerospace capabilities.

Belgium’s selection comes amid broader demand from governments for multi-role aircraft that can be adapted to different mission requirements without significant cost increases.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Constellation Brands beats Q4 consensus but guides FY27 earnings below estimates
Catalyst Bancorp to acquire Lakeside in $41M all-cash deal
data.description:
• Catalyst Bancorp (CLST) will acquire Lakeside Bancshares (LKSB) in an all-cash transaction valued at ~$41.1M.
  • Lakeside shareholders will receive $19.58 per share in cash.
  • The deal includes merging Lakeside into Catalyst and Lakeside Bank into Catalyst Bank, with Catalyst entities surviving.
  • The combined company will have ~$627M in assets, $400M in loans, and $470M in deposits.
  • The acquisition is expected to more than double Catalyst’s size and significantly boost earnings potential.
  • The transaction is projected to be over 180% accretive to EPS and accretive to tangible book value within 3 years.
  • No additional capital is required, and post-merger capital ratios are expected to remain strong.
  • The deal has board approval and is expected to close in Q3 2026.

data.title: Brookdale March occupancy improves in March; Q1 occupancy gains Y/Y
data.description:
Brookdale's (BKD) occupancy improved in March, the operator of senior living communities said on Wednesday.

Month-end occupancy rose to 83.3% in March, up a tick from 83.2% in February and 80.9% in March 2025. 

Same-community weighted average occupancy was 82.5% in March, compared with 82.7% in February and 81.2% in March 2025.

For Q1 2026, weighted average occupancy grew 280 basis points year-over-year to 82.1%, but slipped 40 bps from Q4 2025. Historically, Brookdale's (BKD) Q1 occupancy has declined by an average of 60 bps from the prior quarter, reflecting such factors as winter weather, illnesses, rate changes, and holidays.

The company historically has its strongest occupancy growth period of the year from May through September. 

Brookdale (BKD) stock ticked down 0.1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Trump administration considers punishing some NATO allies - report
data.description:
The Trump administration is weighing a plan to relocate U.S. troops away from NATO countries that opposed the Iran war effort, the Wall Street Journal reported Wednesday, citing administration officials.

The proposal would move U.S. forces from nations deemed unhelpful and station them in more supportive allied countries. While falling short of Trump’s recent threats to fully withdraw from NATO—something he cannot do without Congressional approval—the plan signals deepening tensions between Washington and European allies.

“It’s quite sad that NATO turned their backs on the American people over the last six weeks when it’s the American people who have been funding their defense,” White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Wednesday.

Several allies have drawn administration ire. Spain blocked U.S. planes from its airspace, Italy briefly denied access to an air base in Sicily and German officials criticized the war publicly. The plan could include closing bases in Spain or Germany.

Eastern European nations viewed as supportive—including Poland, Romania, Lithuania, and Greece—could receive additional troops, the WSJ reported. These countries maintain some of the highest defense spending rates in the alliance and quickly approved U.S. requests for base access.

The White House didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comnent.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Northrop Grumman launches U.S. Space Force satellites on Minotaur IV rocket
data.description:
Northrop Grumman (NOC) on Wednesday said it successfully launched multiple satellites for the U.S. Space Force as part of the STP-S29A mission, using its Minotaur IV rocket from Space Launch Complex 8 at Vandenberg Space Force Base in California.

 

The mission marks the 31st successful flight of the Minotaur program since its debut in 2000, according to the company.

 

Upgraded rocket design

The Minotaur IV vehicle is part of a broader family of launch systems developed for U.S. government missions. It uses decommissioned Peacekeeper missile motors supplied by the government, combined with Northrop Grumman’s commercial launch technologies.

The company said recent design changes to the Minotaur IV are intended to simplify manufacturing and mission preparation. These updates reduce the number of components and processes required, while also improving overall performance and enabling faster launch readiness.

Focus on cost, national security missions

The launch supports the U.S. Space Force’s Rocket Systems Launch Program, which is designed to provide flexible and relatively low-cost access to space for government payloads.

Wes Collier, vice president, launch systems, Northrop Grumman (NOC), said in a statement that the mission reflects ongoing efforts to refine the Minotaur IV system and maintain reliable launch capabilities for national security needs, while continuing to adjust the vehicle’s design for future missions.

data.title: PesoRama to raise up to $7 million in private placement
data.description:
• PesoRama (PESO:CA) on Wednesday said that it plans to raise up to $7 million through a non-brokered private placement to fund store expansion and general corporate purposes.
  • The company intends to issue up to 20 million units at $0.35 each, with each unit comprising one common share and half a warrant. 
  • Each full warrant will be exercisable at $0.50 per share for 36 months.
  • PesoRama said it may accelerate the warrant expiry if its shares trade at or above $0.75 for 10 consecutive trading days.
  • Canaccord Genuity will act as a finder for the offering, with potential fees of up to 7% in cash.
  • The company will also pay $25,000 to Markette Ventures for use of its digital investment platform.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about April 21, subject to regulatory approvals, with the securities subject to a four-month holding period.
  • CCORF closed +2.95% at $8.71.
  • Source: Press Release
